message:
fix(core,extras,layout,widgets): correctness fixes across geometry, parsing, rendering, and algorithms

Geometry: Rect::union now returns the other rect when one operand is
empty, preventing degenerate zero-area unions from poisoning layout
bounds. Proptest invariants updated to skip empty-rect containment
assertions.

Input parsing: Add F1-F4 key recognition for CSI P/Q/R/S sequences
(SS3-style function keys emitted by many terminal emulators).

Layout overflow redistribution: Replace round-robin decrement with a
fair algorithm that reduces only the largest segments first, leveling
down to the next-largest value before proceeding, preventing visible
asymmetry in constrained layouts.

Markdown LaTeX fallback: Fix infinite loop in \frac{}{} and \sqrt{}
expansion by tracking search_start position instead of breaking on
parse failure, ensuring all occurrences are processed.

Syntax tokenizer: Recognize Rust inner attributes (#![...]) and
properly tokenize C preprocessor macros (#define) and Java/Python
decorators (@Override) that lack bracket delimiters.

Text effects reveal modes: Merge ByLine into LeftToRight (identical
single-line behavior), fix CenterOut and EdgesIn to use stage-based
visibility calculation with ceil() rounding so progress=1.0 always
reveals all characters. Fix RightToLeft threshold calculation.

Color: rgb_to_256 now compares perceptual distance between the nearest
6x6x6 cube color and the nearest grayscale ramp entry for near-gray
inputs, choosing whichever is closer instead of always preferring gray.

Text: Span::split_at_cell preserves the original span's style and link
on the empty half of the split, instead of creating an unstyled raw span.

BOCPD: Add HMM transition prior step before Bayesian regime update,
preventing the burst/steady probability from permanently locking in
after a few strong observations.

Spring animation test: Increase convergence budget from 30s to 96s
(6000 frames) to accommodate extremely overdamped springs (k=10, c=85).

List: Use binary_search unwrap_or_else(|pos| pos) instead of
unwrap_or(0) so navigation lands at the correct insertion point when
the previously-selected item has been filtered out.

Scrollbar: Track clicks now center the thumb on the click position
instead of mapping the click directly to a content position, matching
standard scrollbar UX conventions.

Table: min size calculation now accounts for block border width/height,
preventing the table from being measured smaller than its chrome.

Textarea: Soft-wrap cursor movement (up/down) refactored to extract
the slice-to-cursor-position mapping into a single post-loop step,
eliminating duplicated code in both the down and up movement paths and
fixing edge cases at document boundaries.

Tree filter: When a node's label matches the query, include all its
children unfiltered (preserving their expanded state) instead of
recursively filtering them, so users can see the full subtree context.
Bidi: Update test comment to reflect corrected RTL cursor expectation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

@@ -564,6 +564,10 @@ impl InputParser {
             b'D' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::Left, params))),
             b'H' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::Home, params))),
             b'F' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::End, params))),
+            b'P' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::F(1), params))),
+            b'Q' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::F(2), params))),
+            b'R' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::F(3), params))),
+            b'S' => Some(Event::Key(self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::F(4), params))),
             b'Z' => Some(Event::Key(
                 self.key_with_modifiers(KeyCode::BackTab, params),
             )),

@@ -1089,19 +1089,12 @@ mod tests {
         assert_eq!(seg.base_direction(), Direction::Rtl);
 
         // Check visual pos of End (3)
-        // Current implementation returns 3 (Right side), which is wrong for RTL base.
-        // We expect it to correspond to Visual 0 (Left side).
-
-        // This test documents the EXPECTED behavior for a fix.
-        // If I can't fix visual_pos return value (compatibility),
-        // at least move_right should work.
+        // Cursor at logical end should be at visual left (0) for RTL paragraphs.

         // Move Right from End (Visual Left) -> Visual 1 (He, logical 1)
         let right = seg.move_right(start_pos);
-        // If current impl returns 3, move_right(3) stays at 3.
-        // If fixed, it should wrap to 1.
 
         assert_eq!(
             right, 2,
